title: Banana fritters
subtitle: Deep-fried banana halves in a coconut and spice batter
excerpt: Banana halves coated in a crisp, spiced coconut batter and deep-fried until golden. A popular street food across Southeast Asia, where the batter is typically seasoned with a pinch of turmeric for colour and rice flour for extra crunch. The banana softens and caramelises inside while the batter sets to a crisp shell. Served with a dusting of icing sugar or with a drizzle of honey.
sourceNotes: Banana fritters (pisang goreng in Malay and Indonesian; kluay tort in Thai) are documented in Southeast Asian culinary literature as a traditional street food preparation. The batter-fried fruit format is described in regional cookery books and domestic food guides from the early twentieth century. British and Dutch colonial cookbooks describing the Southeast Asian kitchen, published before 1928, include battered fried banana as a standard local preparation.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the batter up to 30 minutes ahead. Fry just before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: Deep-fried in vegetable oil at 180°C. Bananas cook quickly; 2 minutes per side is enough for a set, golden exterior and a warm, soft interior.

recipeTools.notes: Deep enough for at least 6 cm of oil.

glossaryTerms.definition: Bananas for frying should be ripe but still firm: yellow with a few small spots, not heavily speckled or soft. Overripe bananas have too much sugar and go mushy inside the batter before the exterior crisps; underripe ones are starchy and flavourless. Slightly firm bananas also hold their shape better when halved and dipped, rather than breaking apart on the skewer or when lowered into the oil. Peel just before battering to prevent browning.

content.text: . The desiccated coconut in the batter gives an extra texture and a slight sweetness; the rice flour makes the batter crisper than plain flour alone. Fry in small batches and serve immediately.

items.prepNote: ripe but firm, peeled and halved lengthways

content.text: Whisk together the plain flour, rice flour, desiccated coconut, sugar, baking powder, and salt. Add the cold water and whisk to a smooth, slightly thick batter. Rest for 15 minutes. Heat the oil to 180°C in a deep saucepan.

Dip each banana half into the batter, coating thoroughly, and let the excess drip off. Lower carefully into the oil and fry in batches of 3 to 4 pieces for 2 minutes per side until golden. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain briefly on paper before transferring to a rack. Dust with icing sugar and serve immediately.
